interactive.rationale: Has some interactivity but the control surface is thin — only a single button/input drives the demo.

references.rationale: The 'see also' section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/4689551782313984 — matches the feature.

design_system.rationale: Design-system audit: overrides a button background without also overriding `color`, the exact regression noted in CLAUDE.md rule #6.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept's lede mirrors the feature index promise — same nouns, same scope.

spec_match.rationale: Concept demonstrates part of the feature but several keywords from the feature-index lede do not appear on the page — likely missing some surface.

edge_cases.rationale: Some failure-mode signalling, but the page largely sticks to the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Broaden the interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: Currently a single control drives the demo. Adding a second axis (e.g. toggle, select, or slider) lets visitors explore a wider slice of the spec — particularly the failure modes.

openQuestions.title: Add a direct spec link
openQuestions.detail: References list only points back to the feature index or ChromeStatus. Add a direct link to the relevant spec (W3C draft, WICG explainer, or RFC) so readers can reach the normative source in one click.

openQuestions.title: Resolve a design-system finding
openQuestions.detail: The page overrides a button background without also overriding `color`, the exact regression noted in CLAUDE.md rule #6. CLAUDE.md rules #5/#6/#7 require CSS variables, paired button background+color overrides, and zero static-card patterns.

openQuestions.title: Cover more of the spec surface
openQuestions.detail: The feature-level lede references API surface that this concept does not appear to demonstrate. Either expand this concept or carve a sibling concept that hits the missing capability.

openQuestions.title: Surface more failure modes
openQuestions.detail: Add a visible branch for the cases this feature can fail in: API missing, permission denied, invalid input, quota exceeded, etc. Right now an unsuccessful call would silently no-op.

summary: Mostly solid but with one structural gap (fail on the rubric). The major open question is the priority fix.
